title: The Boy Nguyen Sinh Cung
summary: Uncle Ho's childhood in Sen Village and Hoang Tru Village, where he learned his first lessons about love for the homeland.

slides.text: Hey kids! Do you know what our beloved Uncle Ho's childhood name was? It was Nguyen Sinh Cung, born into a poor scholarly family in rural Nghe An province.
slides.imagePrompt: A simple thatched house in a village with many lotus flowers (Làng Sen), a young boy with bright eyes sitting on the porch, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: From a young age, Cung listened to his father tell stories of heroes and witnessed French troops mistreating the people. His hatred for the invaders grew from then on.
slides.imagePrompt: Young boy listening to his father by a lamp, shadows of heroes on the wall, serious and thoughtful face, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He was very studious and always curious about the world outside. He wondered: 'Why is our country ruled by others? How can I save my people?'
slides.imagePrompt: Nguyen Sinh Cung reading a book under a banyan tree, looking at the distant mountains, atmosphere of curiosity and purpose, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The moral lessons and love from his family forged a young man with a great heart later in life.
slides.imagePrompt: A warm family portrait, parents and children together, soft glowing light, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What was Uncle Ho's childhood name?
Which famous flower is found in Uncle Ho's paternal hometown?
